name: runtime_session
description: Start, inspect, or stop a daemon-managed Godot runtime session. `start` first runs scene-execution gates: C# projects run `dotnet build`, the scene runs structural validate_scene preflight without a headless runtime pass, and Fennara checks autoload plus scene-attached scripts with targeted diagnostics. If those gates fail, the scene is not opened. Fennara currently allows one managed runtime session globally across all connected Godot editors; if another runtime session is already running, start is blocked and you must call status or stop first. If gates pass, Fennara launches one scene from the selected/bound Godot project in a separate windowed Godot process with the runtime helper enabled. The returned session_id can be reused for multiple runtime_script probes against the same running scene. End the session with runtime_session.stop or with a final runtime_script that calls ctx.close_scene(). The returned runtime_session.log path is the source of truth for scene startup output, runtime errors, runtime_script ctx.log output, capture events, and completion markers. Artifacts and raw stdout/stderr logs are saved under the current tool log directory.

action.description: The runtime session operation. `status` is safe anytime and returns known log/artifact paths. `start` runs preflight gates and then creates a managed logged runtime session only if they pass and no other runtime session is currently running anywhere in the daemon. `stop` closes a running session when you are done or when cleanup is needed. For scene behavior, read the returned runtime_session.log rather than relying on extracted summaries.

scene_path.description: Required for action `start`. The exact `res://` path to a `.tscn` scene to play.

max_run_seconds.description: Optional for action `start`; reserved for future auto-stop behavior. Stop the session explicitly when done.
